content.claim: Sociological fieldwork indicates self-exclusion mechanisms among socially disadvantaged families are driven primarily by the decision to enter and remain in the preparation process and parental strategic decisions, rather than by the entrance exams themselves.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Legal and governance lens
limitations: The relative weight of exam-based selection versus parental self-exclusion is not quantified
Fieldwork methodology and sample size are not provided
This finding contradicts causal mechanisms assumed in other literature; the source of disagreement is not resolved
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
